compiled_state.title: [ticket-platform-compat]: Alpine ticket_create call fails in Docker -> returns empty ID
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: CI failure — Alpine-busybox job in ticket-platform-matrix.yml.
Symptom: ALL ticket_create calls return empty in Alpine 3.19 container. Every Section B and C test fails with 'ticket_create returned id: expected non-empty, actual empty'.
Prior fix attempt: eb730459ea made git rev-parse conditional on TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR being unset and added GIT_DISCOVERY_ACROSS_FILESYSTEM=1. This fix did NOT resolve the issue.
Evidence: CI log shows FAIL on B1, B2, B3, B4, C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, C6, C7 — every single ticket_create call returns empty.
Hypothesis: The failure may be in _ensure_initialized (which always calls git rev-parse without GIT_DISCOVERY_ACROSS_FILESYSTEM=1), or in git commit failing within _flock_stage_commit (Alpine git compat issue), or in clone_ticket_repo failing to set up a valid .tickets-tracker, or safe.directory issues in /tmp subdirs.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 6 (ADVANCED) — CI-only, complex Alpine-specific environment, prior fix eb730459ea failed
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix applied: (1) _flock_stage_commit now probes for util-linux flock via --version grep; non-util-linux (BusyBox) leaves _flock_bin empty → mkdir fallback. (2) GIT_DISCOVERY_ACROSS_FILESYSTEM=1 added to both write_commit_event git rev-parse calls. RED test test-flock-busybox-compat.sh confirms GREEN.
comments.author: Test
